item.title: Citigroup says AI helps speed account openings and systems upgrades
item.description: Citigroup is using artificial intelligence to speed up ‌account openings and the retirement of old software, the U.S. bank's head of technology, Tim Ryan, said, as the firm pushes to improve productivity.

item.title: Berkshire-owned PacifiCorp utility wins ruling that could reduce Oregon wildfire damages
item.description: An Oregon ​appeals court on Wednesday ruled in favor of ‌PacifiCorp in litigation over a series of Oregon wildfires in 2020, saying a trial judge erred ​in letting the case against the ​Berkshire Hathaway-owned utility proceed as a class ⁠action.

item.title: Meta unveils first AI model from costly superintelligence team
item.description: Meta Platforms on Wednesday unveiled Muse Spark, the first artificial intelligence model from a costly team it assembled last year to catch ​up with rivals in the AI race.

item.title: Italy’s Serie A soccer league sounds out private equity for stake in overseas media unit, sources say
item.description: Italy’s Serie ‌A soccer league has sounded out private equity investor interest for a minority stake in its international media rights business, three sources with knowledge of the matter told Reuters.

item.title: Exclusive: US SEC taps Gibson Dunn attorney to be new enforcement director, sources say
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has tapped David Woodcock, ‌a Gibson Dunn lawyer and former agency official, to be its next enforcement director after the regulator's top cop abruptly quit last month, three sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

item.title: Jeff Shell out as Paramount president, Deadline reports
item.description: Jeff ​Shell is out ‌as the president of Paramount Skydance , ​Deadline reported on ​Wednesday.

item.title: Traders place large $950 million bet on oil price falling hours ahead of ceasefire
item.description: Investors placed an ‌approximately $950 million bet on oil prices falling just hours before the U.S. and Iran announced a ceasefire, the latest large wager on the direction of the world's most traded commodity ahead of a major policy announcement by President ​Donald Trump.

item.title: Chinese electric truck maker Windrose makes first US delivery
item.description: Chinese electric truck startup Windrose said ​it had made the ‌first delivery of its long‑haul electric heavy truck in ​the United States, marking ​a key step in ⁠its global expansion.

item.title: Asian stocks exodus fuels largest emerging market outflows since 2020, IIF data shows
item.description: Foreign investors pulled $70.3 billion from emerging market assets in March, the biggest outflow since the pandemic rout in March 2020, data from a global banking trade group showed on Wednesday.

item.title: Aerospace parts maker Arxis targets $11.2 billion valuation in US IPO
item.description: Arxis is targeting a valuation of up to $11.2 billion in its U.S. initial public offering, the aerospace components maker ​said on Wednesday, as defense tech companies take center ‌stage in the listings market amid geopolitical turmoil.

item.title: The new Trump trades: how investors are navigating Iran shocks
item.description: Investors are piecing together a new "Trump trade" playbook for navigating market uncertainty, ranging from whether a U.S.-Iran ceasefire will hold to oil prices staying high for longer.

item.title: FedEx Freight targets up to 12% operating profit growth ahead of spinoff
item.description: FedEx Freight said on Wednesday ​it expects average core profit growth in the ‌range of 10% to 12% over the medium term, ahead of the trucking giant's first-ever investor day as it ​nears a much-anticipated spinoff from its parent.

item.title: Women's elite sports revenues to reach $3 billion in 2026, Deloitte says
item.description: Global revenues in women's elite sports will reach at least $3 billion for the first time ​in 2026, marking an increase of 25% ‌compared to last year, Deloitte said in a report on Wednesday.

item.title: Russia sees China's WeChat, Douyin as models for its homegrown Max messenger
item.description: Russia sees Chinese tech giant Tencent's WeChat messaging app and TikTok sister app Douyin as models for developing its own struggling ​Max messaging app, Vladimir Kiriyenko, CEO of state-controlled internet firm VK , ‌said on Wednesday.

item.title: US mortgage rates edge down to 6.51%, MBA says
item.description: The interest rate on the most popular U.S. home loan ticked down last week for the first ​time since the start of the Iran war, but ‌not enough to rejuvenate a housing market that's pricing out many buyers with a combination of elevated borrowing costs and expensive ​homes.

item.title: Levi shares rise as pricey denim sales smooth over tariff hit
item.description: Levi Strauss' shares jumped about 12% on Wednesday as investors cheered its strongest quarterly revenue growth in nearly four years with confidence growing in the denim maker's ​ability to stave off tariff hits with more full-price sales.

item.title: Exxon signals Q1 upstream profit bump from Iran war, downstream boost in later quarters
item.description: Higher oil and gas ‌prices due to the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could boost Exxon Mobil's first-quarter upstream earnings by up to $2.9 billion, outweighing the impact of disruptions to some of its oil and gas production in the Middle East, the U.S. oil producer said on Wednesday.

item.title: Morning Bid: Big relief rally, for now
item.description: A look at the day ahead in U.S. and global markets from Alun John

item.title: Ackman's UMG bid sees analysts split on Bolloré's next move
item.description: Market analysts are split over whether Universal Music Group's top stakeholders will support billionaire Bill Ackman's Pershing Square proposed $64 billion takeover of the music label.

item.title: Delta pulls growth plans as fuel spike drives up costs, dents Q2 profit
item.description: Delta Air Lines on Wednesday forecast lower-than-expected profit for ​the second quarter and said it was too early to update its full-year outlook, citing uncertainty caused by a run-up in jet fuel prices ‌linked to the Iran war.

item.title: Grab to lean on scale, AI to navigate rising fuel costs, CEO says
item.description: Southeast Asia's top ride-hailing and delivery firm Grab believes artificial intelligence-led products ‌and services will help it drive growth and navigate challenges such as affordability and rising fuel costs in the wake of the war in Iran, company CEO Anthony Tan told Reuters.

item.title: Why the bond market won't bounce back to pre-war levels
item.description: Global bond markets may rebound but are unlikely to fully recover from the war-driven selloff because, even if there is peace, energy prices and inflation will run hotter for longer.

item.title: Fed minutes of March meeting could flesh out how policymakers view war risks to economy
item.description: U.S. Federal Reserve officials knew at their March meeting that the U.S.-Iran war was going to send inflation higher for ​the year, but minutes of the two-day session to be released Wednesday afternoon may flesh out even further the risks policymakers and central ‌bank staff see from a conflict that President Donald Trump has cast in civilizational terms.

item.title: US bank profits to rise on deals, but Iran war fuels outlook uncertainty
item.description: Large U.S. banks are set to post higher quarterly earnings on strong interest income and investment banking fees, analyst estimates show, though investors will focus on ​forecasts as rising geopolitical risks involving Iran add to macroeconomic uncertainty.

item.title: Shah Capital intends to vote against Novavax's board nominees, executive compensation
item.description: Activist investor Shah Capital plans to vote against the re-election of board nominees and the executive ​compensation package at Novavax's upcoming annual meeting, renewing pressure for ‌changes at the vaccine maker.

item.title: Volatility-linked funds put March US stock selling spree in the rearview mirror
item.description: The heaviest stock selling by volatility-linked funds appears to have largely run its course last ‌month, potentially setting up U.S. indexes for gains should market swings subside in coming weeks.

item.title: Tecto plans new Brazil data centers, part of $2 billion investment
item.description: Tecto Data Centers, backed by BTG Pactual's funds, is set to invest $2 billion through 2028, ​including the construction of five data centers in Brazil, the ‌company told Reuters, adding to a wave of new data centers in Latin America's No. 1 economy.

item.title: Swatch urges shareholders to vote against activist investor's bid for board seat
item.description: Swatch has once again opposed GreenWood Investors' bid for a seat on the board and has urged shareholders to vote against ​the activist investor at its upcoming annual general meeting, the ‌watchmaker said on Wednesday.

item.title: Samsung Electronics' shareholder Shinhan Bank launches $2.1 billion share sale - term sheet
item.description: Samsung Electronics shareholder Shinhan ‌Bank launched a sale of about $2.1 billion worth of shares in the South ​Korean company, according to a ​term sheet seen by Reuters on ⁠Wednesday.

item.title: How the math works on a $1.75 trillion SpaceX valuation
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is seeking a $1.75 trillion valuation in its forthcoming initial public offering. How far into the stratosphere is that?
